name: NSE Historical Backtest Collector

# Incrementally builds a multi-year daily-close history for backtesting the
# squeeze/blast strategy per stock. Runs every 15 min, fetches ONE small date
# chunk per run (via the Cloudflare Worker, since only Cloudflare can reach
# NSE's archives reliably), and commits the accumulated data back to the
# repo. Fully resumable — safe to let run for a day or two unattended.
#
# Prerequisite: set the repo secret BACKTEST_WORKER_URL to your deployed
# nse-backtest Cloudflare Worker's base URL, e.g.
#   https://nse-backtest.jami85in.workers.dev
# (Settings -> Secrets and variables -> Actions -> New repository secret)

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "*/15 * * * *"   # every 15 minutes, all day — cheap, no Claude cost
  workflow_dispatch:          # manual trigger to kick off / retry immediately

permissions:
  contents: write

concurrency:
  group: nse-backtest-collect
  cancel-in-progress: false   # never cancel a run — let each chunk finish and commit

jobs:
  collect:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.11"

      - name: Fetch next backtest chunk
        env:
          BACKTEST_WORKER_URL: ${{ secrets.BACKTEST_WORKER_URL }}
          BACKTEST_CHUNK_DAYS: "28"
          BACKTEST_START_DATE: "2020-09-01"
        run: python scripts/backtest_fetch.py

      - name: Commit accumulated data
        run: |
          git config user.name "nse-tracker-bot"
          git config user.email "actions@github.com"
          git add data/backtest/
          git diff --staged --quiet || git commit -m "backtest: chunk collected $(date -u +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC')"
          git pull --rebase --autostash origin main || true
          git push
